text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-off, semis cracked, defensives bid. DIA closed +0.58% while SOXX dumped -5.92%, signaling profit-taking in the AI/semis leadership complex despite still-euphoric 20d SOXX momentum of +19.23% and YTD +88.48%. STYLE showed SMALL > LARGE by 0.06pp, but breadth under the surface skewed to defensives with XLF, XLU, and XLI positive as XLK slid -2.79% and chip headlines around AI demand failed to prevent a positioning clear-out in crowded names like KLAC at -7.44%.

INDICES — VIX slipped to 16.4, below its 20d MA of 17.4, keeping headline vol subdued even as QQQ weakness emerged. Today’s return ranking: DIA +0.58%, GLD +0.27%, SLV -0.13%, IJH -0.30%, RSP -0.33%, IJR -0.54%, SPY -0.60%, IWM -0.87%, QQQ -1.90%, SOXX -5.92%. STYLE printed SMALL > LARGE by 0.06pp, while the standout cross-asset move was USO -4.74% on easing Middle East/Iran tensions and inflation pressure.

VOLUME — SPY volume ran at 1.5x and QQQ at 1.2x, confirming an active, not apathetic, de-risking in broad and growth indices with price down and volume up indicating distribution. Sector-wise, XLC at 1.4x and XLI at 1.1x saw elevated attention as XLK traded at a muted 0.7x, while RSP at 0.6x highlighted low-conviction flows into equal-weight despite the index’s mild -0.33% decline.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLF Financials +1.47%, XLU Utilities +0.72%, XLI Industrials +0.65%, XLB Materials +0.42%, XLRE Real Estate +0.24%, XLP Staples +0.13%, XLC Comms +0.12%, XLV Healthcare +0.03%, XLY Discretionary -0.09%, XLE Energy -0.34%, XLK Technology -2.79%. Top industries were UNG +2.89% and GDX +2.31%, while DRAM -4.15% and SMH -4.81% anchored the downside with SOXX -5.92% confirming broad chip weakness. Flows clearly rotated money out of AI/semis and into financials and defensives, as XLF and XLU outperformed while XLK and XLE lagged despite oil’s collapse. Overall tape fits a defensive risk-off rotation with profit-taking in the AI complex despite ongoing macro-neutral data.

POSITIONING — SPY’s put-call ratio of 0.78 with ATM IV at 12.6% and positive skew of +8.1% shows mild complacency but persistent downside hedging demand versus realized. QQQ’s PCR at 1.61 and skew at +15.0% flag concentrated protection in growth/tech, while its elevated VXN at 26.9 above its 20d MA of 25.5 underscores targeted fear there, and SPY spot drifting toward the 760.0 gamma flip raises sensitivity to further index downside.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil’s -4.74% slide and UUP’s -0.14% drop eased inflation and dollar headwinds, helping financials and defensives even as energy equities underperformed with XLE at -0.34%. Simultaneous gains in TLT at +0.55% and GLD at +0.27% alongside equity leadership from XLF highlight a day of curve-friendly, rate-sensitive bid rather than pure growth chasing.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed printed Greed at 62.8/100, while top movers included UNG +2.89% in commodities, GDX +2.31% in miners, and MCHI +2.71% in China equities, with XLK as the weakest 20d-momentum laggard among majors versus SOXX as the weakest basket today. KLAC’s 5.8x volume spike with price -7.44% signals distribution within “The Fab 7” group, while IVV’s 3.0x spike on -0.57% hints at index-level de-risking. Watch KLAC (-7.44%) for potential follow-through or reversal given the volume capitulation, XLF (+1.47%) for confirmation of value/financial leadership, and REZ (-0.09%) on 3.5x volume for clues on rate-sensitive real estate positioning into the next macro print.
